working_on: Two workstreams: (1) durable independent cross-substrate verification of the S-0193 mempalace retire (audit doc capturing the plan-mode Layer 2 verification: 23/23 byte-equal samples across 7 rooms + diary delta +1 explained + 5 noise drawers documented + SHA-256 backup integrity); (2) close-time defenses to stop the recurring closure-failure pattern empirically observed across S-0184 → S-0193 — add closed_at + status as required fields in audit_archive_structured_fields.py, backfill 4 broken archives (S-0185, S-0187, S-0190, S-0191), author engine/tools/archive_session.py as deterministic step-13 mutation tool, rewire shutdown sequence + SKILL to use it.
declared_scope: Workstream 1: author engine/docs/audits/engine_memory_independent_verification_S-0194.md (the durable Layer 2 audit artifact). Workstream 2: (a) extend audit_archive_structured_fields.py REQUIRED_ARCHIVE_FIELDS with closed_at (str, since S-0001) + status (str with allowed_values [closed, closed_partial], since S-0001) + tests; (b) backfill engine/session/archive/S-0185.json (status), S-0187.json (closed_at), S-0190.json (status+closed_at), S-0191.json (closed_at); (c) author engine/tools/archive_session.py (~150 LOC) + engine/tools/test_archive_session.py with happy/closed_partial/idempotent/refusal cases; (d) rewrite step 13 in engine/operations/session-shutdown-sequence.md + .claude/skills/session-shutdown-sequence/SKILL.md to call the new tool; (e) file 2 follow-up Issues (scan_issue_collisions stopword tuning + psycopg.wait_c root cause). End-to-end verification: the S-0194 close itself uses archive_session.py, the extended audit runs against the resulting archive, the close commit refuses if either new required field is absent. phase: NA-engine
scope_delivery: Fully delivered. Workstream 1: Independent cross-substrate verification audit authored at engine/docs/audits/engine_memory_independent_verification_S-0194.md (verdict PASS — 23/23 byte-equal samples across 6 canonical rooms; 5 not-found general drawers confirmed as documented intentional mempalace closet-chunk noise; diary delta +1 explained by S-0193 escape-hatch entry; SHA-256 backup integrity re-verified; cross-worktree resolver fix confirmed; MCP-boundary FTS5 spot-check across 4 queries returned semantically relevant results). Workstream 2: (a) audit_archive_structured_fields.py REQUIRED_ARCHIVE_FIELDS extended with closed_at + status rows (since_session=S-0194, mode_scope=archive_only), 54/54 tests pass; (b) 4 broken archives backfilled (S-0185 status, S-0187 closed_at, S-0190 both, S-0191 closed_at); (c) engine/tools/archive_session.py authored + 20 pytests pass; (d) step 13 in session-shutdown-sequence.md AND .claude/skills/session-shutdown-sequence/SKILL.md rewritten to call the new tool; (e) Issues #141 (scan_issue_collisions stopword tuning) + #142 (psycopg.wait_c hang root cause, priority:urgent) filed. Mid-session in-band fix: gitignored engine/session/current_engine_memory.jsonl (latent bug; commit b5ad6f8). The S-0194 close itself uses archive_session.py — first-exercise verification.
outcome_summary: S-0194 closed. Two workstreams. WORKSTREAM 1 (mempalace-retire confidence durability): Independent cross-substrate verification authored at engine/docs/audits/engine_memory_independent_verification_S-0194.md — VERDICT PASS. 23/23 byte-equal samples across 6 canonical rooms (decisions, lessons, problems→pushback, foundation→decisions, project→operations, s0003→decisions); 5 'not found' general-room samples investigated and confirmed as mempalace internal closet-chunk word-association indexes (documented intentional drop per S-0193 audit); diary delta +1 explained by the S-0193 escape-hatch entry written via direct Python API; SHA-256 backup integrity re-verified (`d6f23743…`); cross-worktree resolver fix confirmed; MCP-boundary FTS5 spot-check across 4 known-term queries returned semantically relevant top results. WORKSTREAM 2 (close-time defenses for the empirical S-0185/0187/0190/0191 lapse class): root cause located — REQUIRED_ARCHIVE_FIELDS defended outcome_summary_soft_warns/mode/engine_memory_activity/next_session_handle but NOT closed_at or status, the exact two fields the AI manually edits at step 13. Three coupled changes shipped: (1) audit_archive_structured_fields.py REQUIRED_ARCHIVE_FIELDS extended with closed_at + status rows (since_session=S-0194, mode_scope=archive_only via new mode_scope filter — in-flight current.json continues to pass via the filter); (2) 4 broken archives backfilled (S-0185 status, S-0187 closed_at, S-0190 both, S-0191 closed_at — closed_at reconstructed from each session's close-commit committer-date UTC); (3) engine/tools/archive_session.py authored as deterministic step-13 mutation tool (~150 LOC + 20 pytests; idempotent; exit codes 0/2/3 match build/routine_lifecycle_push.py + apply_migration.py convention; closed_at via timestamps.emit() per ADR 0058). Step 13 in session-shutdown-sequence.md and matching SKILL rewired to call the new tool. The S-0194 close itself uses archive_session.py — first-exercise verification (if the tool refuses or the audit hard-fails, the close commit is blocked). Mid-session in-band fix: gitignored engine/session/current_engine_memory.jsonl (latent bug unmasked by plan-mode MCP calls pre-eager-claim; commit b5ad6f8). Two follow-up Issues filed: #141 (scan_issue_collisions stopword tuning — deferred T3-C gate reached) and #142 (psycopg.wait_c hang root cause — S-0186 connect_timeout=10 was necessary but not sufficient; pgbouncer strips session-level options=-c, priority:urgent).
